commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a gateway helper to recombine non-streaming final and `/btw` reply payload chunks without trimming indentation, plus regression tests for split YAML, authored single-newline boundaries, and CRLF boundaries.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +79. Total +96 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main still trims payload text in the `/btw`, final transcript, and assistant display text builders before recombining chunks; the contributor also provided real setup screenshots showing the indentation-sensitive output after the fix.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated refresh or replacement PR can carry the useful fix onto current main's gateway text aggregation paths.

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve indentation when recombining non-streaming replies
target.body:
AI-assisted: yes (OpenClaw agent-authored).
Testing: targeted local validation (gateway regression tests + targeted lint + pre-commit repo checks).

## Summary

- Companion to merged #55960, not a duplicate: #55960 fixed indentation loss while stripping inline reply directives in `src/utils/directive-tags.ts`; this PR fixes the remaining gateway-layer loss when non-streaming reply parts are recombined in `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`.
- Preserve each non-streaming payload chunk as authored when recombining it, so indentation-sensitive content survives payload boundaries.
- Reuse that same combiner for `/btw` side results so sibling non-streaming gateway paths behave consistently.
- Normalize `CRLF` / `CR` / `LF` boundary trimming and add focused regression coverage for the single-newline, `/btw`, and CRLF cases.
- Scope boundary: no streaming-path formatting changes, no Markdown parsing changes, and no channel-specific formatting rules.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #42576
- Related #29377
- Companion to merged #55960

## Relationship to #55960

Merged PR #55960 fixed one real whitespace-loss layer: inline reply-directive stripping in the shared parser (`src/utils/directive-tags.ts`).

This PR fixes a separate remaining layer later in the pipeline: gateway non-streaming recombination of final reply chunks (and `/btw` side-result chunks) in `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`.

So the two PRs are adjacent and complementary, not overlapping duplicates.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Non-streaming final replies and `/btw` side results now preserve indentation when formatted content spans multiple payloads. When one chunk already ends with a single newline and the next chunk does not start with one, the gateway now preserves that authored single newline instead of forcing a blank line. The combiner also trims `CRLF` / `CR` / `LF` cleanly at outer boundaries so Windows-style line endings do not leak stray carriage returns.

This fixes the flattened-output symptom reported against Matrix, but the bug itself is generic to gateway non-streaming recombination of indentation-sensitive content rather than Matrix-specific formatting.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local Node.js checkout
- Model/provider: n/a (gateway-side reply recombination)
- Integration/channel (if any): reproduced against the gateway non-streaming `chat.send` path; user report was on Matrix
- Relevant config (redacted): n/a

### Steps

1. Produce a non-streaming assistant reply whose formatted block is emitted across multiple final payloads.
2. Include indentation-sensitive content such as nested YAML, a nested list, or a fenced code block.
3. Observe the stored/broadcast final message text.

### Expected

- Indentation from each payload chunk is preserved in the final combined reply.

### Actual

- Leading whitespace at payload boundaries was trimmed away, flattening structured content.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Added focused regression coverage for:
- the authored single-newline separator case
- an indentation-sensitive `/btw` YAML side result
- `CRLF` boundary trimming

The linked user issue includes before/after screenshots of the real-world symptom.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: targeted gateway regression test for the single-newline recombination edge case; targeted `/btw` side-result regression test for an indented YAML block; targeted `CRLF` boundary regression coverage; targeted lint on the changed files; repo pre-commit checks during commit.
- Edge cases checked: whitespace-only payload chunks are still ignored; separator insertion still only happens when adjacent chunks are not already separated by line breaks; authored single-newline separators are preserved instead of expanded to blank lines; `CRLF` boundaries do not leave stray carriage returns.
- What you did **not** verify: live Matrix delivery against a running instance, broader streaming reply behavior outside the existing `/btw` block-side-result coverage, or full-repo test suites.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert commits `6b2461a11`, `c753cd0af`, and `d667f18f1` (or revert the PR branch changes as a unit)
- Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`, and `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: unexpected extra blank lines between merged payload chunks, unexpected loss of an authored single newline between chunks, stray carriage returns from `CRLF` boundaries, or lost indentation in `/btw` side results

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: preserving authored separators changes the old trim/join behavior for payloads that already end in a single newline.
  - Mitigation: this is intentional, explicitly regression-tested, and still bounded by the same whitespace-only-chunk skipping plus separator insertion only when neither side already provides a line break.
- Risk: preserving chunk text could expose `CRLF` edge cases at outer boundaries.
  - Mitigation: the combiner now trims `CRLF` / `CR` / `LF` consistently at the start/end, with targeted regression coverage.
- Risk: reviewers may mistake this PR for a duplicate of #55960.
  - Mitigation: this description now names the exact layer split: `#55960` fixes directive parsing, this PR fixes gateway non-streaming recombination.
target.author: vrurg

commands.author: ghitafilali

target.title: fix: restart gateway after isolated cron setup timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- classify isolated cron setup timeouts as a gateway-health failure class
- notify the gateway cron service when setup stalls before the isolated runner starts
- request a safe gateway restart through the existing restart coordinator so affected recurring jobs can recover centrally
- add cron-service and gateway regression coverage for the restart path

## Root Cause
Cron jobs that use isolated agent turns can fail before the runner starts with `cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start`. Today that error is recorded and alerted, but it is not treated as a platform-level gateway/isolated-runner health failure. Individual cron workflows can self-repair around it, but the shared failure mode keeps recurring across unrelated jobs.

## Impact
When this failure class occurs, OpenClaw now asks the existing safe-restart coordinator to restart only after the normal preflight drain says it is safe. This should reduce repeated user-visible cron failure cards without interrupting active work.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Recurring isolated-agent cron jobs were repeatedly failing before runner start while the gateway was stale/hot, producing `cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start` cards instead of recovering centrally.
- **Real environment tested:** Real local macOS OpenClaw setup, LaunchAgent-managed local gateway on loopback, released OpenClaw `2026.5.28`, with active background workload.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Verified the PR head `354de1a3b99027027b303c29b8852ec8fef9091d`; ran the PR-head proof command `pnpm test src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/infra/restart-coordinator.test.ts src/infra/infra-runtime.test.ts --reporter verbose`; ran the production build command `node scripts/build-all.mjs`; then ran the built-runtime proof command `node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-pr-89055-setup-timeout-proof.mjs`. I also checked the real recovered machine with `openclaw gateway status`, `openclaw status --json`, and `ps -p 93467 -o pid,pcpu,pmem,rss,etime,command`.
- **PR-build restart proof:** `node scripts/build-all.mjs` passed on PR head `354de1a3b99027027b303c29b8852ec8fef9091d`. The built-runtime proof then ran a controlled setup stall through the real cron timeout path and the real safe restart scheduler. Its output showed `productionBuildCompleted=true`, `distContainsGatewayHook=true`, `elapsedMs=60282`, `result.error="cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start"`, `restartRequests[0].status="scheduled"`, `restartRequests[0].preflightSafe=true`, `restartRequests[0].restartCoalesced=false`, `restartRequests[0].restartMode="emit"`, `restartRequests[0].restartReason="cron.isolated_agent_setup_timeout"`, `sigusr1Events[0].authorized=true`, `sigusr1Events[0].recovered=true`, and all four assertions true: setup timeout result, safe restart scheduled, gateway run loop recovered, and production dist contains the gateway hook.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the recovered real machine showed `openclaw gateway status` with `Runtime: running (pid 93467, state active)`, `Connectivity probe: ok`, `Capability: admin-capable`, and `Gateway version: 2026.5.28`. Copied live `openclaw status --json` output showed `gateway.reachable=true`, `gateway.connectLatencyMs=45`, `queuedSystemEvents=[]`, and `tasks.byStatus.queued=0`. Copied live `ps` output showed gateway pid `93467` around `1.0%` CPU, `3.2%` memory, RSS `534560` KB. Before recovery, the same setup had a stale gateway timeout and a gateway node process around `109%` CPU with roughly `2.8GB` RSS. Supplemental PR-head terminal output also showed `✓ |gateway| ... requests a safe gateway restart when isolated cron setup times out`, `✓ |infra| ... coalesces duplicate scheduled restarts into a single pending timer`, and `✓ |infra| ... defers SIGUSR1 until deferral check returns 0`.
- **Observed result after fix:** The real gateway recovered and stayed reachable instead of continuing the setup-timeout loop. Normal OpenClaw work resumed through the restored gateway: representative background work and inference processes were running successfully after the gateway recovered. On PR head, the selected proof run passed gateway `22/22`, infra `34/34`, and cron `43/43`, proving this patch requests the same safe restart path for the setup-timeout class while the existing coordinator coalesces/defers repeated restart requests.
- **What was not tested:** The exact PR build was not installed into a live released OpenClaw package; the built-runtime proof exercises the PR-head cron timeout path and real safe restart scheduler in-process, while the live machine proof covers the operational recovery path on the released install.

## Testing
- `pnpm test src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/infra/restart-coordinator.test.ts src/infra/infra-runtime.test.ts --reporter verbose`
- `pnpm test src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `git diff --check`

Note: `pnpm check:changed` was also attempted locally before this PR, but the local Blacksmith Testbox wrapper failed its own `--version/--help` sanity check before project checks could run.
target.author: ghitafilali

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists

commands.command: re-review\n\nadded pr-head proof to the real behavior proof section: verbose gateway/cron/infra test output now shows the setup-timeout callback requests safe restart, and the existing restart layer coalesces/defers repeated restart requests.
commands.freeform_prompt: re-review\n\nAdded PR-head proof to the Real behavior proof section: verbose gateway/cron/infra test output now shows the setup-timeout callback requests safe restart, and the existing restart layer coalesces/defers repeated restart requests.

Note: `pnpm check:changed` was also attempted locally before this PR, but the local Blacksmith Testbox wrapper failed its own `--version/--help` sanity check before project checks could run.
